medical_history.chief_complaint: Hemifacial asymmetry with flattening of the right forehead and limited opening of the mouth with trismus
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: A few months after birth, her parents noticed hemifacial asymmetry with flattening of the right forehead and limited opening of the mouth with trismus. At the age of 1 year, the patient was treated for TMJ arthrosis in another center, where she underwent surgery consisting in masseter muscle weakening. This operation was unsuccessful.
medical_history.past_medical_history: TMJ arthrosis, masseter muscle weakening surgery at age 1
medical_history.family_history: No history of trauma to the mandible or familiar occurrence of similar abnormalities

physical_examination.general_examination: Mandibular lock with incisal clearance of 5 mm, associated with extremely limited protrusion and lateral mandibular movements. On palpation of the masticatory muscles, the masseter was found to be enlarged and very tense.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Preoperative three-dimensional CT reconstructions showed an enlarged coronoid process protruding at least 1 cm above the inferior rim of the zygomatic arch, accompanied by bone sclerosis. The zygomatic bone had an abnormal shape with an excess in the lower part.
